sanskrit: असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम्। अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते॥
transliteration: asaṃśayaṃ mahā-bāho mano dur-nigrahaṃ calam | abhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa ca gṛhyate ||
literal_meaning: Undoubtedly, O mighty-armed one, the mind is difficult to curb and restless; yet, O son of Kuntī, it is brought under control by practice and by dispassion.
contextual_meaning: Krishna validates Arjuna’s frustration—mental regulation is objectively hard—then offers a two-factor protocol: repeated skillful action (abhyāsa) plus systematic disengagement from emotionally charged outcomes (vairāgya).
psychological_state: Post-validation readiness—after having his struggle normalized, the client moves from despair to a structured, evidence-based plan for attentional control.
trigger_scenario: A professional tells her therapist, 'I can’t stop doom-scrolling; my mind is broken,' and hears, 'Brains are wired that way; daily mindfulness plus cutting the reward-loop will re-wire it.'
anchor_text: Train the mind daily and let go of craving outcomes.
